page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.10xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 40 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.4 GB of memory per core. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, the instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on external network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier across several key categories. The dedicated Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1439.29 and a multi-core score of 38381.56. In memory-intensive tasks, cached read-only small block bandwidth reaches 2132.58 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write bandwidths are 105.52 GB/sec and 36.66 GB/sec respectively. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates a Geekbench multi-core score of 11735.0, with notable performance in software compilation at 28295.0 and ray tracing at 30187.0. It also processes 34.66 million Redis SET operations per second and handles static web serving throughput at 23.2 GB/sec.

The ecs.hfc6.10xlarge offers a high-compute density profile, making it qualitatively cost-effective for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. However, the lack of bundled local storage means users must factor in the latency and cost of attached block storage. The 1:2.4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff, prioritizing raw processing power over memory capacity. This configuration is well-suited for compute-heavy applications such as software compilation, web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and moderate database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.10xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 40 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 3.1 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture under a KVM hypervisor, it provides 96.0 GB of RAM, translating to 2.4 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on external storage solutions and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate solid compute capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 38381.56 and a Redis SET throughput of 34.66 million operations per second. This hardware configuration is designed for CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing where high-frequency dedicated cores are prioritized over large memory footprints or local storage.
